behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels". This correctness depends on real AI/ML diarization output from the speech-to-text model; a stubbed transcript can hide real speaker-label failures (LLM-DEP + MOCK-HIDE). Rejected higher level (Workflow): behavior text focuses on one model output property (speaker labels), not multiple model decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real multi-speaker meeting audio through the real speech-to-text API and assert transcript segments include usable speaker labels that allow attendee attribution downstream.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: Escalation check quote: "Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments ... into the summary input structure." This is deterministic parsing/structuring logic at a local function/object boundary. No real OS/network/subprocess and no dependence on model judgment are required.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative transcript lines into the parser function and assert extracted objects contain correct assignee and task text fields, including edge cases like multiple assignees and punctuation.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee...". This is about LLM output completeness/coverage, so a deterministic mock can always pass while hiding real generation misses (LLM-DEP + MOCK-HIDE). Rejected higher level (Workflow): this behavior can be validated with a fixed transcript into one real summarization step; multiple real agentic stages are not required by the text.
behaviors.test_description: Provide a transcript with explicit assignments for multiple attendees to the real summarization model and assert each assigned attendee appears with at least one action item in the structured output.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Escalation check quote: "Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema". This is deterministic routing/mapping logic and can fail within one local boundary without I/O or model dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Test the routing function with pre-identified decision records and assert they are written to `decisions` schema fields and excluded from generic narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section". This depends on real model extraction/placement quality; mocks hide whether the model actually surfaces decisions vs narrative drift (LLM-DEP + deterministic-assertion trap). Rejected higher level (Workflow): behavior can be tested with one transcript-to-summary model call; text does not require multiple agentic decisions in sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Run real summarization on transcripts containing clear decisions and assert those decisions are present in the `decisions` section and absent as only narrative-only mentions.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary." This is a multi-step pipeline with repeated model-involved stages where handoff quality across steps matters (MULTI-STEP + INTEGRATION-BRAKE(d)). A mock can validate orchestration but hide real coherence degradation across segment merges.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real >60-minute meeting recording, run full segmented processing end-to-end with real model calls per stage, and assert final merged summary is structurally complete and semantically consistent across segment boundaries.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: Escalation quote: "full pipeline ... transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output ... for a real meeting recording." This explicitly requires end-to-end execution across multiple model-dependent stages and infrastructure boundaries, so lower-level tests can miss cross-stage failures.
behaviors.test_description: Execute a production-like end-to-end run on a real meeting file: upload, real transcription API call, real summarization call, and output delivery; assert successful completion and valid structured payload without human fixes.
